feedback.text: Dear Sir or Madam, I am a concered citizen who is deeply troubled with the digital products are treated around this world. We can see that throughout the recent years digital products have not been treated with the same care as physical products. We have seen software being advertised and sold even though the company has already stated the servers will be closed within the year, rendering the software useless. We have seen apps make millions on lootboxes only to turn the servers off leaving the customers with nothing but the bills. I am of course talking about the games "The Crew" and the lesser known "Final Fantasy Record Keeper", These are but only two examples of hundreds of games that have been "killed", made inaccessable to the consumer by their companies. The Digital Fairness act should definitely concern itself with these types of abuses of digital products. Just because the products do not exists in physical form does not give the companies the right to dictate on how we use them or even if can use them. A product that can be taken away at any point is not owned. Thank you for your time. Best regards Andre Pries
